title: Peloton Reports Q3 FY2026 Earnings: Revenue Beats Guidance, Quarterly Profit, Subscribers Decline
text: Peloton Q3 FY2026 Earnings Analysis. Revenue Performance: Peloton exceeded expectations with total revenue of $630.9 million, surpassing both their guidance range ($605-$625M) and analyst expectations ($618.7M). This marked the first year-over-year revenue growth Peloton has reported in some time, driven by stronger connected fitness equipment sales. Profitability: The company posted GAAP net income of $26.4 million ($0.06 per share), representing significant improvement from the $47.7 million loss in the prior-year quarter. This was their third profitable quarter in four quarters. Adjusted EBITDA reached $126 million (up 41% YoY), while free cash flow climbed to $151 million (up 59% YoY). Subscriber Trends: Connected fitness subscribers declined to 2.662 million, down 7.6% year-over-year, though in line with company projections. However, subscription revenue increased to $428 million (up 4% sequentially), and monthly churn improved to 1.2%. Financial Position: Net debt decreased significantly to $173 million, down 70% from $585 million annually, enabling next phase of strategic capital allocation. Forward Guidance: Full-year revenue guidance $2.42-$2.44 billion; Free cash flow ~$350 million (raised $75 million); Adjusted EBITDA $470-$480 million.
